text:
The search results unambiguously confirm this is a well-known, real book. The automated checker's "mismatch" (attributing it to "L. Werner" in 2016) clearly reflects the checker matching a completely different work — perhaps a 2016 paper or book with a similar title by a different author. The cited reference (Ronald A. Fisher, *The Design of Experiments*, Oliver & Boyd, Edinburgh, 1935) is one of the most famous statistical works in history, and is confirmed by Wikipedia, a Nature review, Google Books, Internet Archive, and multiple bookseller listings.

EXPLANATION: This is the famous 1935 book by Ronald A. Fisher, confirmed by Wikipedia, Nature's contemporary review, Google Books, and Internet Archive; the automated checker's mismatch reflects it matching a different, unrelated work by "L. Werner" (2016). The cited title, author, publisher, and year are all fully verified.
LINK: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Design_of_Experiments
FOUND_TITLE: The Design of Experiments
FOUND_AUTHORS: Ronald A. Fisher
FOUND_YEAR: 1935
VERDICT: UNLIKELY
